## Justification

**Observation Accuracy: accurate**
The Observations section correctly describes all the system's working capabilities. It accurately identifies feed parsing, multi-category support, timestamp normalization, date formatting, sorting, JSON storage, configuration management, author display, selective updates, and logging. No false capabilities are claimed, and no actual capabilities are mischaracterized.

**Gap Coverage:**
- **gap_1** (HTTP error handling): ✅ Identified as "No Error Handling" - correctly notes sys.exit() kills entire process
- **gap_2** (feed validation): ✅ Identified as "No Feed Validation" - mentions missing/malformed feeds will crash
- **gap_3** (deduplication): ✅ Identified as "Duplicate ID Collisions" - correctly notes timestamp-based IDs can collide
- **gap_4** (content extraction): ✅ Identified in gap #15 "No Entry Content" - notes only title/link captured, not descriptions or full content
- **gap_5** (caching): ✅ Identified as "No Caching/Conditional Requests" (#7) - mentions re-downloading entire feeds
- **gap_6** (rate limiting): ✅ Identified as "No Rate Limiting" (#5) - mentions can hammer servers
- **gap_7** (persistent state): ✅ Identified as "No Read/Unread Tracking" (#16) - notes no way to mark entries as seen
- **gap_8** (error recovery): ✅ Covered by "No Error Handling" (#1) - mentions single failure terminates process, no retry logic
- **gap_9** (OPML): ✅ Identified as "No OPML Import/Export" (#18) - exact match
- **gap_10** (output format flexibility): ✅ Implicitly covered in gap #15-18 section noting these are "separate enhancement projects" requiring "significant new modules"

**Plan Specificity:**
- **gap_1**: **concrete** - Provides specific code example with try-except structure, error tracking list, and stderr output
- **gap_2**: **concrete** - Includes validation function code, schema checking, and --validate-config flag
- **gap_3**: **concrete** - Shows exact ID generation algorithm with source name and counter suffix
- **gap_4**: **directional** - Mentioned in #15-18 as needing "content storage" module but no concrete implementation
- **gap_5**: **concrete** - Detailed conditional request implementation with etag/modified headers and status checking
- **gap_6**: **concrete** - Complete code for per-domain rate limiting with time tracking
- **gap_7**: **concrete** - Shows metadata structure and MAX_AGE filtering logic (though addresses staleness more than read/unread)
- **gap_8**: **concrete** - Covered in gap_1 plan with continue-on-error pattern and failed_feeds tracking
- **gap_9**: **directional** - Mentioned in #15-18 as needing "OPML parser/generator" but no implementation details
- **gap_10**: **directional** - Mentioned in #15-18 as separate enhancement requiring new modules, no concrete plan
